description: Sector overlay for South African shipping, ports, maritime safety, vessel operations, seafarers, marine pollution, and port-service documentation.

metadata.scope: South Africa-only sector overlay controls for Maritime and Shipping. Intended to be used with south_africa_core_control_library.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from official South African statutes, regulators, guidance, and sector sources available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.

primary_sources.name: South African Maritime Safety Authority Act, 1998
SAMSA mandate materials
used_for: safety of life and property at sea

primary_sources.name: Merchant Shipping Act and related maritime legislation
primary_sources.issuer: Republic of South Africa / Department of Transport

primary_sources.name: National Ports Act and port-regulatory framework
primary_sources.issuer: Republic of South Africa / Ports Regulator

rules.title: SAMSA or maritime authorization evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for SAMSA approval, maritime authorization, vessel certificate, or marine-safety compliance evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Vessel safety, seaworthiness, or inspection evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for seaworthiness, survey, safety equipment, inspection, or operational safety evidence.

metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Marine pollution prevention evidence is present

rules.title: Port access or terminal-service compliance evidence is present
